title: baphia dewevrei subsp. dewevrei
intro: Baphia dewevrei subsp. dewevrei, also known as the Baphia tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Tanzania, Kenya, and the Democratic Republic of Congo. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases and promote overall well-being. In terms of herbal preparations, Baphia dewevrei subsp. dewevrei roots are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
